title: Beginner Applied Mathematics: Quantitative Decisions
description: Adults and secondary-level learners rebuilding mathematical confidence through practical decisions, measurement, data, finance, and entry-level computational modeling. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate quantitative literacy through introductory algebra through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate measurement, finance, probability, statistics, geometry, and logic through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate spreadsheet and python-supported applied modeling through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning.
misconceptions: A bare number has the same meaning in every context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning.

competencies.statement: Estimate magnitudes with benchmarks and defend an appropriate tolerance before calculating exactly.
misconceptions: An estimate is merely an uneducated guess.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate magnitudes with benchmarks and defend an appropriate tolerance before calculating exactly.

competencies.statement: Translate a practical question into known quantities, unknown quantities, constraints, and operations.
misconceptions: The first visible number always determines the first operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate a practical question into known quantities, unknown quantities, constraints, and operations.

competencies.statement: Check signs, scale, units, and limiting cases to reject unreasonable numerical answers.
misconceptions: Calculator output is automatically a valid answer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Check signs, scale, units, and limiting cases to reject unreasonable numerical answers.

competencies.statement: State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.
misconceptions: Assumptions should remain hidden so an answer looks certain.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.

competencies.statement: Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
misconceptions: Every decimal is rational and every square root is irrational.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.

competencies.statement: Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
misconceptions: Moving a decimal point does not change scale when zeros are added.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.

competencies.statement: Model direction and change with signed numbers and explain each operation on a number line.
misconceptions: A negative sign always means a quantity is physically bad.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model direction and change with signed numbers and explain each operation on a number line.

competencies.statement: Evaluate grouped expressions with a documented order of operations and intermediate checks.
misconceptions: Operations are performed strictly from left to right.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate grouped expressions with a documented order of operations and intermediate checks.

competencies.statement: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision.
misconceptions: Rounding every intermediate value improves accuracy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision.

competencies.statement: Represent fractions as part-whole, quotient, ratio, operator, and location meanings in context.
misconceptions: A fraction always describes a piece of one physical object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent fractions as part-whole, quotient, ratio, operator, and location meanings in context.

competencies.statement: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions by reasoning about units and equivalent forms.
misconceptions: Numerators and denominators can always be combined independently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions by reasoning about units and equivalent forms.

competencies.statement: Convert among fractions, decimals, and terminating or repeating representations without losing meaning.
misconceptions: A longer decimal representation is necessarily more precise.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert among fractions, decimals, and terminating or repeating representations without losing meaning.

competencies.statement: Distinguish ratios, rates, and unit rates and use units to choose a valid comparison.
misconceptions: Every comparison should be made by subtracting totals.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish ratios, rates, and unit rates and use units to choose a valid comparison.

competencies.statement: Test proportionality with tables, graphs, equations, and constant ratios.
misconceptions: Two quantities are proportional whenever both increase.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test proportionality with tables, graphs, equations, and constant ratios.

competencies.statement: Identify the comparison base before calculating a percent, percentage, or whole.
misconceptions: The percent base is whichever number appears first.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify the comparison base before calculating a percent, percentage, or whole.

competencies.statement: Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.
misconceptions: A change of ten units always means a ten percent change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.

competencies.statement: Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.
misconceptions: A twenty percent increase and twenty percent decrease cancel.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.

competencies.statement: Model repeated percent change with compounding rather than repeated addition.
misconceptions: Repeated rates always apply to the original amount.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model repeated percent change with compounding rather than repeated addition.

competencies.statement: Detect denominator switching, percentage-point confusion, and selective baselines in public claims.
misconceptions: Percent and percentage-point differences are interchangeable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect denominator switching, percentage-point confusion, and selective baselines in public claims.

competencies.statement: Choose appropriate SI base and derived units and write their symbols correctly.
misconceptions: Unit capitalization and spacing never affect technical meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose appropriate SI base and derived units and write their symbols correctly.

competencies.statement: Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.
misconceptions: A conversion is safe whenever the numerical value looks familiar.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Convert quantities with unit factors that equal one and cancel dimensions explicitly.

competencies.statement: Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.
misconceptions: Area units use the same linear conversion factor as length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Square or cube conversion factors when converting area and volume.

competencies.statement: Distinguish resolution, accuracy, precision, tolerance, and measurement uncertainty.
misconceptions: More decimal places guarantee a more accurate measurement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish resolution, accuracy, precision, tolerance, and measurement uncertainty.

competencies.statement: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.
misconceptions: Units are decorative labels added after the arithmetic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.

competencies.statement: Define variables with units and domains rather than treating letters as unlabeled blanks.
misconceptions: A variable may change meaning midway through a solution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define variables with units and domains rather than treating letters as unlabeled blanks.

competencies.statement: Substitute values into expressions with grouping, units, and intermediate reasonableness checks.
misconceptions: Substitution means deleting parentheses around negative values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Substitute values into expressions with grouping, units, and intermediate reasonableness checks.

competencies.statement: Solve one-variable linear equations while preserving equality at every transformation.
misconceptions: Moving a term across the equals sign changes it by magic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve one-variable linear equations while preserving equality at every transformation.

competencies.statement: Rearrange literal formulas to isolate a required quantity and verify dimensions.
misconceptions: A formula can only be used in the arrangement first shown.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rearrange literal formulas to isolate a required quantity and verify dimensions.

competencies.statement: Model limits and feasible ranges with inequalities, including sign reversal under negative scaling.
misconceptions: An inequality behaves exactly like an equation under every operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model limits and feasible ranges with inequalities, including sign reversal under negative scaling.

competencies.statement: Classify angles and two- and three-dimensional shapes by defining properties rather than appearance.
misconceptions: Rotating a shape changes its mathematical classification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify angles and two- and three-dimensional shapes by defining properties rather than appearance.

competencies.statement: Choose and justify perimeter or area models for borders, surfaces, and composite regions.
misconceptions: Perimeter and area are interchangeable measures of size.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and justify perimeter or area models for borders, surfaces, and composite regions.

competencies.statement: Choose volume or surface-area models for capacity, covering, and packaging tasks.
misconceptions: Doubling every length merely doubles volume.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose volume or surface-area models for capacity, covering, and packaging tasks.

competencies.statement: Use a consistent scale factor to move between drawings and actual dimensions.
misconceptions: A scale drawing can use different scale factors on each axis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a consistent scale factor to move between drawings and actual dimensions.

competencies.statement: Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold.
misconceptions: The Pythagorean theorem applies to every triangle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold.

competencies.statement: Classify fixed, variable, periodic, and discretionary cash flows in a time-aligned budget.
misconceptions: A monthly budget can ignore annual and irregular costs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify fixed, variable, periodic, and discretionary cash flows in a time-aligned budget.

competencies.statement: Calculate simple interest and distinguish principal, rate, and time units.
misconceptions: An annual rate can be multiplied by any time value without conversion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Calculate simple interest and distinguish principal, rate, and time units.

competencies.statement: Model compound growth by period and distinguish nominal from effective change.
misconceptions: Compound interest adds the same dollar amount every period.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model compound growth by period and distinguish nominal from effective change.

competencies.statement: Compare payment, total repayment, term, fees, and opportunity cost across loan offers.
misconceptions: The smallest monthly payment always identifies the cheapest loan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare payment, total repayment, term, fees, and opportunity cost across loan offers.

competencies.statement: Perform sensitivity checks on income, rate, and expense assumptions before recommending a plan.
misconceptions: A financial forecast should present one certain future.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Perform sensitivity checks on income, rate, and expense assumptions before recommending a plan.

competencies.statement: Represent categories, subsets, unions, intersections, and complements from precise definitions.
misconceptions: Categories that overlap must be counted as disjoint.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent categories, subsets, unions, intersections, and complements from precise definitions.

competencies.statement: Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.
misconceptions: Adding group totals always gives the union total.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.

competencies.statement: Distinguish statements, predicates, quantifiers, negation, converse, inverse, and contrapositive.
misconceptions: A statement and its converse always have the same truth value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish statements, predicates, quantifiers, negation, converse, inverse, and contrapositive.

competencies.statement: Build truth tables to test compound conditions and policy rules.
misconceptions: The word or always means exactly one condition can be true.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build truth tables to test compound conditions and policy rules.

competencies.statement: Find unreachable, overlapping, and contradictory cases in multi-condition decision rules.
misconceptions: A rule is complete if its common case works.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Find unreachable, overlapping, and contradictory cases in multi-condition decision rules.

competencies.statement: Define outcomes and sample spaces before assigning probabilities.
misconceptions: Possible outcomes are automatically equally likely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define outcomes and sample spaces before assigning probabilities.

competencies.statement: Use complement, addition, and multiplication rules with explicit event relationships.
misconceptions: Probabilities of any two events can always be multiplied.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use complement, addition, and multiplication rules with explicit event relationships.

competencies.statement: Interpret conditional probability as a changed reference group.
misconceptions: Conditional probability merely reverses the wording of an event.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret conditional probability as a changed reference group.

competencies.statement: Use tables, trees, multiplication, permutations, or combinations according to order and replacement.
misconceptions: Permutations and combinations answer the same counting question.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use tables, trees, multiplication, permutations, or combinations according to order and replacement.

competencies.statement: Compute and interpret expected value without claiming it predicts a single outcome.
misconceptions: Expected value is the outcome most likely to occur once.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute and interpret expected value without claiming it predicts a single outcome.

competencies.statement: Formulate a statistical question that anticipates variability and names a population.
misconceptions: A question with one fixed answer is automatically statistical.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Formulate a statistical question that anticipates variability and names a population.

competencies.statement: Classify variables and evaluate whether a sample can represent the target population.
misconceptions: A large convenience sample removes selection bias.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify variables and evaluate whether a sample can represent the target population.

competencies.statement: Choose tables and graphs whose encodings fit the variable and comparison.
misconceptions: Any graph type communicates any dataset equally well.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose tables and graphs whose encodings fit the variable and comparison.

competencies.statement: Compare mean, median, mode, range, quartiles, and standard deviation in context.
misconceptions: The mean is always the most representative center.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare mean, median, mode, range, quartiles, and standard deviation in context.

competencies.statement: Identify misleading scales, omitted groups, confounding, and correlation-causation errors.
misconceptions: A strong association proves that one variable caused the other.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify misleading scales, omitted groups, confounding, and correlation-causation errors.

competencies.statement: Plot and interpret ordered pairs with axis quantities, units, origin, and scale.
misconceptions: Swapping coordinates leaves a plotted relationship unchanged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plot and interpret ordered pairs with axis quantities, units, origin, and scale.

competencies.statement: Determine whether a relationship is a function and define practical input and output domains.
misconceptions: Every curved graph fails to represent a function.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Determine whether a relationship is a function and define practical input and output domains.

competencies.statement: Translate a relationship among words, tables, graphs, and formulas while preserving meaning.
misconceptions: Equivalent representations must look visually similar.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate a relationship among words, tables, graphs, and formulas while preserving meaning.

competencies.statement: Interpret slope as a signed rate with output units per input unit.
misconceptions: Slope is only a geometric steepness number without units.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret slope as a signed rate with output units per input unit.

competencies.statement: Interpret intercepts and restrict conclusions to the meaningful domain of a model.
misconceptions: Every intercept has a practical real-world meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret intercepts and restrict conclusions to the meaningful domain of a model.

competencies.statement: Use relative and absolute cell references intentionally in copied formulas.
misconceptions: Copied formulas always keep the intended reference.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use relative and absolute cell references intentionally in copied formulas.

competencies.statement: Add input constraints, units, labels, and audit cells to a spreadsheet model.
misconceptions: A spreadsheet is self-explanatory because formulas are visible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Add input constraints, units, labels, and audit cells to a spreadsheet model.

competencies.statement: Express a unit-aware calculation as readable Python variables, functions, and output.
misconceptions: Code removes the need to define variables and assumptions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express a unit-aware calculation as readable Python variables, functions, and output.

competencies.statement: Test normal, boundary, invalid, and independently calculated cases.
misconceptions: One matching example proves a calculation tool is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test normal, boundary, invalid, and independently calculated cases.

competencies.statement: Publish inputs, method, version, results, limitations, and rerun instructions.
misconceptions: A screenshot is sufficient evidence that a model can be reproduced.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish inputs, method, version, results, limitations, and rerun instructions.

competencies.statement: Iterate through question, assumptions, variables, model, computation, validation, and communication.
misconceptions: Modeling is a one-pass calculation after choosing a formula.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Iterate through question, assumptions, variables, model, computation, validation, and communication.

competencies.statement: Compare candidate models and choose the simplest one that preserves material behavior.
misconceptions: The most complicated available model is automatically best.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare candidate models and choose the simplest one that preserves material behavior.

competencies.statement: Change material inputs and assumptions to identify which conclusions remain stable.
misconceptions: A result that looks precise does not need sensitivity analysis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Change material inputs and assumptions to identify which conclusions remain stable.

competencies.statement: State scope, limitations, uncertainty, and data gaps without weakening useful conclusions.
misconceptions: Limitations should be hidden because they make work look less confident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State scope, limitations, uncertainty, and data gaps without weakening useful conclusions.

competencies.statement: Defend a recommendation with calculations, visual evidence, alternatives, and clear next actions.
misconceptions: Showing arithmetic alone communicates a complete recommendation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend a recommendation with calculations, visual evidence, alternatives, and clear next actions.

credential.title: Beginner Applied Mathematics: Quantitative Decisions Certificate
